prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh sweetie, you know how your brain tells your whole body what to do? Well, the CPU is kind of like the brain of the computer! It's a tiny little chip tucked inside the machine that thinks through all the instructions and figures out what needs to happen next. When you click on something or type a letter, that little chip is working away like crazy to make it all happen. I don't fully understand all the ins and outs of it myself, but I do know that without it, the whole computer just wouldn't work at all, kind of like how we couldn't do much without our own noggins! Isn't it something, the things they can fit into such a tiny little piece?
